title: Free diaper samples — just got mine in the mail!
body: Wanted to share — I requested free diaper samples from Pampers a couple weeks ago and they finally arrived today. Totally free, no shipping charge, no credit card needed. Just filled out a short form on their site and they mailed a pack of newborn and size 1 diapers. Highly recommend doing this before baby arrives so you can find which brand works best!
author: MamaOf2Soon
label: pass
label_reason: Genuinely free physical diaper samples mailed to home with zero shipping cost and no payment required.
notes: Clear pass — classic sample request with no payment barrier

title: Similac sent me a huge formula sample box for free!
body: I signed up on the Similac Strong Moms website when I was about 20 weeks and just got a big box of formula samples in the mail — completely free. The box included three different sample cans so you can try different formulas before committing to a big purchase. No shipping fee, nothing to pay. If you're pregnant or newly postpartum I'd sign up ASAP!
author: FirstTimeFTM2026
label: pass

tier2_expected.item: baby formula sample box (multiple sample cans)

label_reason: Free physical baby formula sample box mailed with no shipping cost or payment required.
notes: Classic formula sample program — well-known pass case

title: Did anyone else get the free baby welcome bag from their hospital?
body: We just got home from the hospital and they gave us the most amazing free welcome bag — full of baby samples, a onesie, wipes, a small formula sample, nipple cream, and a few other goodies. Totally free, it's part of their new mom program. If your hospital is partnered with Pampers or Similac they often have these welcome boxes. No cost whatsoever!
author: NewMomTired
label: pass

tier2_expected.item: baby welcome bag (onesie, wipes, formula sample, nipple cream, baby samples)

label_reason: Free physical baby welcome box given at hospital with no cost or shipping charge.
notes: Hospital welcome bag — physical goods, zero cost, clear pass

title: Reminder: you can get a FREE breast pump through insurance — here's how
body: Just wanted to remind everyone that the ACA requires insurance to cover a breast pump at zero cost to you. I used Aeroflow Breastpumps and they handled all the insurance paperwork and shipped a Spectra S2 to my door for free. No shipping cost, nothing out of pocket. You just fill out a form and they verify your coverage. I got mine about 3 weeks before my due date. So worth doing!
author: PreggoAdvice
label: pass
label_reason: Free breast pump shipped to door via insurance program with zero out-of-pocket cost including shipping.
notes: Insurance-covered breast pump — physical item, fully free delivery, clear pass

title: Gerber is sending free baby food samples — no catch!
body: I just got my free Gerber baby food sample pack in the mail! I signed up through their website a few months back and forgot about it, then this box showed up with several pouches of different purees and a small jar. Completely free, they paid shipping too. My LO is only 3 months but I'm excited to try these when she's ready for solids. Link is on their website under 'baby's first foods.'
author: MamaBearAZ
label: pass

tier2_expected.item: baby food sample pack (puree pouches and jar)

label_reason: Free physical baby food samples mailed to home with free shipping and no payment required.
notes: Baby food sample program — real physical goods, free delivery

title: 50% off diaper coupon code at Target — expires Sunday!
body: Hey mamas! Target has a promo code for 50% off Huggies diapers this weekend only. Use code DIAPER50 at checkout. You still have to pay half price so it's not totally free but it's a great deal. Works on any size. I just stocked up and saved like $30! Sharing because we all need diapers lol.
author: DealHunterMom
label: reject
label_reason: Coupon code requiring payment — not genuinely free, user still pays 50% of the cost.
notes: Discount coupon — clearly a reject, requires payment

title: FREE 30-day trial of Hello Bello diaper subscription
body: Hello Bello is offering a free 30-day trial of their diaper subscription service. You do need to enter a credit card but they won't charge you until the trial ends. You can cancel anytime before day 30 to pay nothing. The diapers are really cute and the quality is great — this is a good way to try them risk-free before committing to a subscription!
author: SubscriptionMommy
label: reject
label_reason: Free trial requiring a credit card — not genuinely free, has a time limit and requires cancellation to avoid charges.
notes: Subscription trial with credit card required — reject per trial exclusion rule

title: Enter to win a FREE UPPAbaby stroller! Sweepstakes ends April 30
body: Babylist is running a sweepstakes where you can win a UPPAbaby Vista V2 stroller — total value $1,200! Just enter your email on their site. No purchase necessary but winners are chosen randomly so there's no guarantee. Drawing is April 30th. Good luck everyone — this would be amazing to win for a new baby!
author: StrollerDreams
label: reject
label_reason: Sweepstakes — not guaranteed to receive, luck-based, no certainty of getting the item.
notes: Classic sweepstakes reject — random winner selection

title: Free lactation consultant virtual appointment — worth it!
body: I just wanted to share that my insurance covers a free virtual lactation consultant session through a telehealth platform. It's completely free with most insurance plans. The consultant helped me so much with my latch issues and answered all my questions. If you're struggling with breastfeeding this is totally worth looking into — completely free service! Not a physical product but an amazing resource.
author: BreastfeedingAdvocate
label: reject
label_reason: Free service (consultation) — not a physical good, Tier 1 rejects non-physical offers.
notes: Service, not physical goods — even the author notes 'not a physical product'

title: Buy One Get One FREE baby wipes at Costco this month
body: Costco members — Kirkland wipes are BOGO free right now through the end of the month. You have to buy one pack to get the second one free, so it works out to 50% off each pack. It's a great deal if you're stocking up on wipes! You need a Costco membership to take advantage. We grabbed four packs and it felt amazing to stock up the nursery.
author: CostcoMommy
label: reject
label_reason: Buy-one-get-one deal — requires purchase of first item, not genuinely free.
notes: BOGO requires initial purchase — reject per 'requires purchase' rule
